data.title: Is ANZ’s (ASX:ANZ) Quiet Equity Housekeeping Hinting at a Deeper Shift in Capital Discipline?
data.description:
• ANZ Group Holdings has reported the lapse and cessation of multiple ANZAA options and rights that expired on 22 November 2025, alongside a correction that 217,974 units were actually forfeited under a prior arrangement, refining its recorded issued capital.
  • While these adjustments have little direct effect on ordinary shareholders, they highlight ANZ’s ongoing housekeeping around equity incentives as investor attention shifts toward upcoming Australian jobs, inflation and central bank decisions.
  • With ANZ’s recent capital housekeeping in the background, we’ll explore how heightened focus on RBA-sensitive data affects its investment narrative.

ANZ Group Holdings Investment Narrative Recap

To own ANZ Group Holdings, you need to be comfortable with a large, regulated bank that is reshaping itself around digital platforms while remaining sensitive to Australian interest rate decisions. The recent tidy up of ANZAA options and a correction to forfeited units appears immaterial to that bigger picture, with the near term focus still on RBA driven shifts in margins and the risk that slower economic data or higher credit provisions weigh on earnings momentum.

The most relevant recent disclosure here is ANZ’s clarification that 217,974 units were actually forfeited under a prior arrangement, which fine tunes its recorded issued capital. While small in scale, it sits alongside earlier updates on digital investment and cost control, reminding investors that execution on ANZ Plus and Transactive, and the timing of related cost savings, remains central to how the interest rate backdrop feeds into returns.

Yet investors should be aware that if ANZ’s heavy technology spend fails to deliver the planned efficiencies and customer growth, then …

data.title: Could Intel’s Tiber AI Integration Recast PDF Solutions (PDFS) as a Core Semiconductor Data Platform?
data.description:

The Intel Tiber AI Studio integration supports the near term demand catalyst for Exensio, but it does not materially change the key risk that heavy investment and a still developing SaaS style model can pressure margins if customer spending slows.

Among recent developments, the reaffirmed 2025 full year revenue growth guidance of 21% to 23% year over year ties most closely to this news. Stronger traction for Exensio, supported by the Intel Tiber AI Studio integration, sits directly behind that top line goal and highlights how dependent the near term story is on successfully scaling higher value software and data contracts.

data.title: Is NIO (NIO) Finally Fairly Priced After Years Of Share Price Declines?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether NIO at around US$4.71 is a bargain or a value trap, you are not alone, many investors are asking the same question.
  • The stock has seen a mixed return profile, with a 7 day return of 2.9% decline, a 30 day return of 5.6% decline, a year to date return of 8.4% decline, a 1 year return of 8.0%, a 3 year return of 60.9% decline, and a 5 year return of 92.1% decline.
  • Recent headlines around NIO have continued to focus on its position in the electric vehicle space and investor debate over how to interpret its share price performance. This context matters because sentiment around the sector can quickly spill over into how the market prices individual names such as NIO.
  • Our valuation checks currently give NIO a score of 2 out of 6. Next we will break that down across different valuation approaches before finishing with a broader way of thinking about what NIO might really be worth.

NIO scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: NIO Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows, then discounts them back to today to arrive at an estimate of what the business might be worth per share.

For NIO, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach based on cash flow projections. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of around CN¥19,679.27m. Analysts provide explicit forecasts for the next few years, and Simply Wall St extrapolates further out, with projected free cash flow of CN¥9,666m in 2030. All of these CN¥ figures are then discounted back to today using the model assumptions.

On this basis, the estimated intrinsic value is about US$4.89 per share, compared with a recent share price of around US$4.71. That implies the stock is about 3.6% undervalued, which is a very small gap and could easily be closed by normal market moves.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

NIO is fairly valued according to our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF), but this can change at a moment's notice. Track the value in your watchlist or portfolio and be alerted on when to act.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for NIO.

Approach 2: NIO Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings are weak or volatile, the P/S ratio is often more useful than P/E because it compares the share price to revenue rather than profit, which can swing around with investment cycles and one off items.

In general, higher growth expectations or lower perceived risk can justify a higher P/S multiple. Slower expected growth or higher risk usually line up with a lower, more conservative range.

NIO currently trades on a P/S of 1.14x. That sits above the Auto industry average of 0.75x, but below the peer group average of 2.36x. Simply Wall St also calculates a Fair Ratio for NIO of 1.06x, which is the P/S level suggested by factors such as its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is more tailored than a simple comparison with peers or the wider industry because it adjusts for NIO’s own characteristics rather than assuming all Auto stocks deserve the same multiple.

Compared with the Fair Ratio of 1.06x, NIO’s actual 1.14x P/S is only slightly higher, which points to the shares being priced about in line with those fundamentals.

Result: ABOUT RIGHT

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1443 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your NIO Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation. Let us introduce you to Narratives, which let you set out your story for NIO, link that story to your own revenue, earnings and margin assumptions, and then turn it into a fair value that you can easily compare to today’s share price. All of this happens within Simply Wall St’s Community page, where Narratives update automatically as new news or earnings arrive. One investor might build a bullish NIO Narrative around future chip licensing revenue, delivery growth across NIO, ONVO and FIREFLY, and a fair value closer to US$9.00. Another might focus on weaker near term volume guidance, softer 2026 demand expectations, higher competition and a more cautious fair value near US$3.00. This gives you a clear, side by side view of how different views on the same company translate into different estimates of what the stock might be worth and when it could be attractive or expensive for you personally.

Do you think there's more to the story for NIO? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Subdued Growth No Barrier To MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:MTSI) With Shares Advancing 27%
data.description:
Despite an already strong run, MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ:MTSI) shares have been powering on, with a gain of 27% in the last thirty days. The last 30 days bring the annual gain to a very sharp 52%.

Since its price has surged higher, MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings may be sending strong sell signals at present with a price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 17.1x, when you consider almost half of the companies in the Semiconductor industry in the United States have P/S ratios under 5.6x and even P/S lower than 2x aren't out of the ordinary. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the highly elevated P/S.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Check out our latest analysis for MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings

Advertisement

What Does MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings' Recent Performance Look Like?

MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings could be doing better as it's been growing revenue less than most other companies lately. One possibility is that the P/S ratio is high because investors think this lacklustre revenue performance will improve markedly. If not, then existing shareholders may be very nervous about the viability of the share price.

Want the full picture on analyst estimates for the company? Then our free report on MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings will help you uncover what's on the horizon.

Do Revenue Forecasts Match The High P/S Ratio?

In order to justify its P/S ratio, MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings would need to produce outstanding growth that's well in excess of the industry.

If we review the last year of revenue growth, the company posted a terrific increase of 32%. The latest three year period has also seen an excellent 43% overall rise in revenue, aided by its short-term performance. Therefore, it's fair to say the revenue growth recently has been superb for the company.

Turning to the outlook, the next year should generate growth of 16% as estimated by the analysts watching the company. Meanwhile, the rest of the industry is forecast to expand by 45%, which is noticeably more attractive.

With this in consideration, we believe it doesn't make sense that MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings' P/S is outpacing its industry peers. Apparently many investors in the company are way more bullish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock at any price. There's a good chance these shareholders are setting themselves up for future disappointment if the P/S falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

The Final Word

Shares in MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings have seen a strong upwards swing lately, which has really helped boost its P/S figure. We'd say the price-to-sales ratio's power isn't primarily as a valuation instrument but rather to gauge current investor sentiment and future expectations.

It comes as a surprise to see MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings trade at such a high P/S given the revenue forecasts look less than stellar. The weakness in the company's revenue estimate doesn't bode well for the elevated P/S, which could take a fall if the revenue sentiment doesn't improve. Unless these conditions improve markedly, it's very challenging to accept these prices as being reasonable.

Having said that, be aware MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings is showing 1 warning sign in our investment analysis, you should know about.

If these risks are making you reconsider your opinion on MACOM Technology Solutions Holdings, explore our interactive list of high quality stocks to get an idea of what else is out there.

data.title: Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) Is Increasing Its Dividend To CA$1.67
data.description:
Bank of Montreal (TSE:BMO) will increase its dividend from last year's comparable payment on the 26th of February to CA$1.67. Even though the dividend went up, the yield is still quite low at only 3.5%.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Bank of Montreal's Dividend Forecasted To Be Well Covered By Earnings

It would be nice for the yield to be higher, but we should also check if higher levels of dividend payment would be sustainable.

Having distributed dividends for at least 10 years, Bank of Montreal has a long history of paying out a part of its earnings to shareholders. Past distributions do not necessarily guarantee future ones, but Bank of Montreal's payout ratio of 56% is a good sign as this means that earnings decently cover dividends.

Looking forward, EPS is forecast to rise by 40.3% over the next 3 years. The future payout ratio could be 44% over that time period, according to analyst estimates, which is a good look for the future of the dividend.

Check out our latest analysis for Bank of Montreal

Bank of Montreal Has A Solid Track Record

The company has an extended history of paying stable dividends. Since 2016, the dividend has gone from CA$3.20 total annually to CA$6.68. This means that it has been growing its distributions at 7.6% per annum over that time. Dividends have grown at a reasonable rate over this period, and without any major cuts in the payment over time, we think this is an attractive combination as it provides a nice boost to shareholder returns.

Bank of Montreal Could Grow Its Dividend

Investors could be attracted to the stock based on the quality of its payment history. It's encouraging to see that Bank of Montreal has been growing its earnings per share at 9.0% a year over the past five years. The company is paying a reasonable amount of earnings to shareholders, and is growing earnings at a decent rate so we think it could be a decent dividend stock.

We Really Like Bank of Montreal's Dividend

Overall, we think this could be an attractive income stock, and it is only getting better by paying a higher dividend this year. Earnings are easily covering distributions, and the company is generating plenty of cash. All of these factors considered, we think this has solid potential as a dividend stock.

It's important to note that companies having a consistent dividend policy will generate greater investor confidence than those having an erratic one. Meanwhile, despite the importance of dividend payments, they are not the only factors our readers should know when assessing a company. Companies that are growing earnings tend to be the best dividend stocks over the long term. See what the 13 analysts we track are forecasting for Bank of Montreal for free with public analyst estimates for the company. Looking for more high-yielding dividend ideas? Try our collection of strong dividend payers.

data.title: Have Applied Digital Insiders Been Selling Stock?
data.description:
Some Applied Digital Corporation (NASDAQ:APLD) shareholders may be a little concerned to see that the Chairman, Wesley Cummins, recently sold a substantial US$6.0m worth of stock at a price of US$36.42 per share. However, that sale only accounted for 7.0% of their holding, so arguably it doesn't say much about their conviction.

This technology could replace computers: discover the 20 stocks are working to make quantum computing a reality.

Advertisement

Applied Digital Insider Transactions Over The Last Year

In fact, the recent sale by Chairman Wesley Cummins was not their only sale of Applied Digital shares this year. They previously made an even bigger sale of -US$6.1m worth of shares at a price of US$15.26 per share. That means that even when the share price was below the current price of US$37.40, an insider wanted to cash in some shares. When an insider sells below the current price, it suggests that they considered that lower price to be fair. That makes us wonder what they think of the (higher) recent valuation. While insider selling is not a positive sign, we can't be sure if it does mean insiders think the shares are fully valued, so it's only a weak sign. This single sale was just 13% of Wesley Cummins's stake.

Applied Digital insiders didn't buy any shares over the last year. The chart below shows insider transactions (by companies and individuals) over the last year. If you want to know exactly who sold, for how much, and when, simply click on the graph below!

View our latest analysis for Applied Digital

For those who like to find hidden gems this free list of small cap companies with recent insider purchasing, could be just the ticket.

Insider Ownership

For a common shareholder, it is worth checking how many shares are held by company insiders. A high insider ownership often makes company leadership more mindful of shareholder interests. Applied Digital insiders own 6.0% of the company, currently worth about US$625m based on the recent share price. I like to see this level of insider ownership, because it increases the chances that management are thinking about the best interests of shareholders.

What Might The Insider Transactions At Applied Digital Tell Us?

Insiders haven't bought Applied Digital stock in the last three months, but there was some selling. And there weren't any purchases to give us comfort, over the last year. While insiders do own a lot of shares in the company (which is good), our analysis of their transactions doesn't make us feel confident about the company. While it's good to be aware of what's going on with the insider's ownership and transactions, we make sure to also consider what risks are facing a stock before making any investment decision. In terms of investment risks, we've identified 3 warning signs with Applied Digital and understanding these should be part of your investment process.

Of course Applied Digital may not be the best stock to buy. So you may wish to see this free collection of high quality companies.

For the purposes of this article, insiders are those individuals who report their transactions to the relevant regulatory body. We currently account for open market transactions and private dispositions of direct interests only, but not derivative transactions or indirect interests.

data.title: WH Smith PLC (LON:SMWH) Stock Goes Ex-Dividend In Just Two Days
data.description:
Some investors rely on dividends for growing their wealth, and if you're one of those dividend sleuths, you might be intrigued to know that WH Smith PLC (LON:SMWH) is about to go ex-dividend in just 2 days. Typically, the ex-dividend date is two business days before the record date, which is the date on which a company determines the shareholders eligible to receive a dividend. The ex-dividend date is an important date to be aware of as any purchase of the stock made on or after this date might mean a late settlement that doesn't show on the record date. Accordingly, WH Smith investors that purchase the stock on or after the 22nd of January will not receive the dividend, which will be paid on the 12th of February.

The company's upcoming dividend is UK£0.06 a share, following on from the last 12 months, when the company distributed a total of UK£0.17 per share to shareholders. Last year's total dividend payments show that WH Smith has a trailing yield of 2.7% on the current share price of UK£6.295. Dividends are a major contributor to investment returns for long term holders, but only if the dividend continues to be paid. So we need to investigate whether WH Smith can afford its dividend, and if the dividend could grow.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Dividends are typically paid from company earnings. If a company pays more in dividends than it earned in profit, then the dividend could be unsustainable. WH Smith lost money last year, so the fact that it's paying a dividend is certainly disconcerting. There might be a good reason for this, but we'd want to look into it further before getting comfortable. Considering the lack of profitability, we also need to check if the company generated enough cash flow to cover the dividend payment. If WH Smith didn't generate enough cash to pay the dividend, then it must have either paid from cash in the bank or by borrowing money, neither of which is sustainable in the long term. Luckily it paid out just 22% of its free cash flow last year.

Check out our latest analysis for WH Smith

Click here to see the company's payout ratio, plus analyst estimates of its future dividends.

Advertisement

Have Earnings And Dividends Been Growing?

Companies with consistently growing earnings per share generally make the best dividend stocks, as they usually find it easier to grow dividends per share. Investors love dividends, so if earnings fall and the dividend is reduced, expect a stock to be sold off heavily at the same time. WH Smith was unprofitable last year, but at least the general trend suggests its earnings have been improving over the past five years. Even so, an unprofitable company whose business does not quickly recover is usually not a good candidate for dividend investors.

The main way most investors will assess a company's dividend prospects is by checking the historical rate of dividend growth. WH Smith has seen its dividend decline 7.1% per annum on average over the past 10 years, which is not great to see.

Remember, you can always get a snapshot of WH Smith's financial health, by checking our visualisation of its financial health, here.

The Bottom Line

From a dividend perspective, should investors buy or avoid WH Smith? We're a bit uncomfortable with it paying a dividend while being loss-making. However, we note that the dividend was covered by cash flow. It might be worth researching if the company is reinvesting in growth projects that could grow earnings and dividends in the future, but for now we're not all that optimistic on its dividend prospects.

In light of that, while WH Smith has an appealing dividend, it's worth knowing the risks involved with this stock. Every company has risks, and we've spotted 2 warning signs for WH Smith you should know about.

If you're in the market for strong dividend payers, we recommend checking our selection of top dividend stocks.

data.title: Tango Therapeutics, Inc.'s (NASDAQ:TNGX) 53% Price Boost Is Out Of Tune With Revenues
data.description:
Tango Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ:TNGX) shareholders have had their patience rewarded with a 53% share price jump in the last month. This latest share price bounce rounds out a remarkable 356% gain over the last twelve months.

Following the firm bounce in price, Tango Therapeutics' price-to-sales (or "P/S") ratio of 26.3x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to other companies in the Biotechs industry in the United States, where around half of the companies have P/S ratios below 12.1x and even P/S below 4x are quite common. However, the P/S might be quite high for a reason and it requires further investigation to determine if it's justified.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 15 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

See our latest analysis for Tango Therapeutics

Advertisement

What Does Tango Therapeutics' P/S Mean For Shareholders?

Tango Therapeutics could be doing better as it's been growing revenue less than most other companies lately. It might be that many expect the uninspiring revenue performance to recover significantly, which has kept the P/S ratio from collapsing. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

Keen to find out how analysts think Tango Therapeutics' future stacks up against the industry? In that case, our free report is a great place to start.

How Is Tango Therapeutics' Revenue Growth Trending?

Tango Therapeutics' P/S ratio would be typical for a company that's expected to deliver very strong growth, and importantly, perform much better than the industry.

Taking a look back first, we see that the company grew revenue by an impressive 53% last year. Pleasingly, revenue has also lifted 175% in aggregate from three years ago, thanks to the last 12 months of growth. So we can start by confirming that the company has done a great job of growing revenue over that time.

Shifting to the future, estimates from the nine analysts covering the company suggest revenue should grow by 0.2% per annum over the next three years. With the industry predicted to deliver 126% growth each year, the company is positioned for a weaker revenue result.

With this information, we find it concerning that Tango Therapeutics is trading at a P/S higher than the industry. Apparently many investors in the company are way more bullish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock at any price. There's a good chance these shareholders are setting themselves up for future disappointment if the P/S falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

The Final Word

Shares in Tango Therapeutics have seen a strong upwards swing lately, which has really helped boost its P/S figure. Using the price-to-sales ratio alone to determine if you should sell your stock isn't sensible, however it can be a practical guide to the company's future prospects.

We've concluded that Tango Therapeutics currently trades on a much higher than expected P/S since its forecast growth is lower than the wider industry. The weakness in the company's revenue estimate doesn't bode well for the elevated P/S, which could take a fall if the revenue sentiment doesn't improve. At these price levels, investors should remain cautious, particularly if things don't improve.

We don't want to rain on the parade too much, but we did also find 3 warning signs for Tango Therapeutics that you need to be mindful of.

If you're unsure about the strength of Tango Therapeutics' business, why not explore our interactive list of stocks with solid business fundamentals for some other companies you may have missed.

data.title: Some Confidence Is Lacking In DT Midstream, Inc.'s (NYSE:DTM) P/E
data.description:
DT Midstream, Inc.'s (NYSE:DTM) price-to-earnings (or "P/E") ratio of 30.4x might make it look like a strong sell right now compared to the market in the United States, where around half of the companies have P/E ratios below 19x and even P/E's below 11x are quite common. Nonetheless, we'd need to dig a little deeper to determine if there is a rational basis for the highly elevated P/E.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

DT Midstream could be doing better as its earnings have been going backwards lately while most other companies have been seeing positive earnings growth. One possibility is that the P/E is high because investors think this poor earnings performance will turn the corner. You'd really hope so, otherwise you're paying a pretty hefty price for no particular reason.

See our latest analysis for DT Midstream

If you'd like to see what analysts are forecasting going forward, you should check out our free report on DT Midstream.
Advertisement

Is There Enough Growth For DT Midstream?

DT Midstream's P/E ratio would be typical for a company that's expected to deliver very strong growth, and importantly, perform much better than the market.

If we review the last year of earnings, dishearteningly the company's profits fell to the tune of 3.6%. At least EPS has managed not to go completely backwards from three years ago in aggregate, thanks to the earlier period of growth. So it appears to us that the company has had a mixed result in terms of growing earnings over that time.

Looking ahead now, EPS is anticipated to climb by 12% per year during the coming three years according to the nine analysts following the company. With the market predicted to deliver 12% growth each year, the company is positioned for a comparable earnings result.

In light of this, it's curious that DT Midstream's P/E sits above the majority of other companies. Apparently many investors in the company are more bullish than analysts indicate and aren't willing to let go of their stock right now. These shareholders may be setting themselves up for disappointment if the P/E falls to levels more in line with the growth outlook.

The Bottom Line On DT Midstream's P/E

Typically, we'd caution against reading too much into price-to-earnings ratios when settling on investment decisions, though it can reveal plenty about what other market participants think about the company.

We've established that DT Midstream currently trades on a higher than expected P/E since its forecast growth is only in line with the wider market. Right now we are uncomfortable with the relatively high share price as the predicted future earnings aren't likely to support such positive sentiment for long. This places shareholders' investments at risk and potential investors in danger of paying an unnecessary premium.

It's always necessary to consider the ever-present spectre of investment risk. We've identified 1 warning sign with DT Midstream, and understanding should be part of your investment process.

Of course, you might also be able to find a better stock than DT Midstream. So you may wish to see this free collection of other companies that have reasonable P/E ratios and have grown earnings strongly.

data.title: Returns Are Gaining Momentum At CEMEX. de (BMV:CEMEXCPO)
data.description:
If we want to find a potential multi-bagger, often there are underlying trends that can provide clues. One common approach is to try and find a company with returns on capital employed (ROCE) that are increasing, in conjunction with a growing amount of capital employed. This shows us that it's a compounding machine, able to continually reinvest its earnings back into the business and generate higher returns. So when we looked at CEMEX. de (BMV:CEMEXCPO) and its trend of ROCE, we really liked what we saw.

We've found 21 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

What Is Return On Capital Employed (ROCE)?

If you haven't worked with ROCE before, it measures the 'return' (pre-tax profit) a company generates from capital employed in its business. The formula for this calculation on CEMEX. de is:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.064 = US$1.4b ÷ (US$29b - US$6.5b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Thus, CEMEX. de has an ROCE of 6.4%. In absolute terms, that's a low return and it also under-performs the Basic Materials industry average of 9.2%.

See our latest analysis for CEMEX. de

Above you can see how the current ROCE for CEMEX. de compares to its prior returns on capital, but there's only so much you can tell from the past. If you'd like, you can check out the forecasts from the analysts covering CEMEX. de for free.

What Can We Tell From CEMEX. de's ROCE Trend?

CEMEX. de has not disappointed with their ROCE growth. Looking at the data, we can see that even though capital employed in the business has remained relatively flat, the ROCE generated has risen by 21% over the last five years. Basically the business is generating higher returns from the same amount of capital and that is proof that there are improvements in the company's efficiencies. On that front, things are looking good so it's worth exploring what management has said about growth plans going forward.

What We Can Learn From CEMEX. de's ROCE

To sum it up, CEMEX. de is collecting higher returns from the same amount of capital, and that's impressive. And with a respectable 81% awarded to those who held the stock over the last five years, you could argue that these developments are starting to get the attention they deserve. With that being said, we still think the promising fundamentals mean the company deserves some further due diligence.

While CEMEX. de looks impressive, no company is worth an infinite price. The intrinsic value infographic for CEMEX CPO helps visualize whether it is currently trading for a fair price.

For those who like to invest in solid companies, check out this free list of companies with solid balance sheets and high returns on equity.

data.title: Do Shifting Analyst Views On American Tower’s (AMT) Growth Reset Its Core Investment Narrative?
data.description:
• In recent days, American Tower has been the focus of multiple analyst revisions that reassessed its growth outlook amid mixed macroeconomic conditions and evolving tower-leasing expectations ahead of its upcoming earnings report.
  • While some analysts highlighted sector-specific risks such as lower leasing tied to EchoStar and comparisons with fixed income, they also pointed to improving supply-demand fundamentals and ongoing expectations for earnings and revenue growth, underscoring how closely the company is being watched.
  • Next, we’ll examine how this reassessment of American Tower’s growth outlook ahead of earnings shapes its broader investment narrative for investors.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 22 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

What Is American Tower's Investment Narrative?

To own American Tower, you have to be comfortable with a relatively steady, infrastructure-like business that still lives and dies by leasing growth and the health of carrier spending. The recent cuts to analyst price targets, tied to EchoStar-related leasing caution and comparisons with fixed income, temper the previous growth narrative but do not overturn it. Short term, the key catalysts remain upcoming earnings, any revision to leasing and property revenue guidance, and management’s commentary on demand across U.S. and international towers. The biggest near term risks now look more tilted toward softer new leasing and a slower economy than the earlier analysis assumed, even as improving supply and demand fundamentals and a still-healthy dividend track record provide some counterbalance. Recent share gains suggest the market sees this reset as manageable rather than thesis breaking.

However, the leasing risk linked to EchoStar is something investors should not ignore. American Tower's shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 31%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Five Simply Wall St Community fair value estimates span roughly US$195 to US$265, showing wide disagreement on upside. Set that against softer leasing expectations and macro uncertainty, and you can see why opinions on American Tower’s future performance differ so much.

data.title: Ubtech Robotics (SEHK:9880) Is Up 9.5% After Airbus Humanoid Robot Deal - Has The Bull Case Changed?
data.description:
• Ubtech Robotics recently secured an order from Airbus to deploy its industrial humanoid robots in the aircraft maker’s manufacturing facilities, building on prior industrial automation collaborations.
  • This agreement, alongside over ¥1.40 billion in humanoid robot orders reported for 2025, underscores growing adoption of Ubtech’s technology across complex production lines.

What Is Ubtech Robotics' Investment Narrative?

To own Ubtech Robotics, you have to believe that humanoid robots can become a real, repeatable product in industrial automation, not just a showcase. The Airbus order fits that thesis neatly, reinforcing the earlier Texas Instruments collaboration and giving more substance to the reported ¥1.40 billion in humanoid orders for 2025. In the short term, that may support the existing revenue growth story and help justify a rich price to sales multiple, but it does not yet solve the core issues of large ongoing losses and reliance on fresh equity. With the share price already strong and repeated share issuances over 2024–2025, the key question is whether contracts like Airbus meaningfully shift the path toward scale and operating leverage, or simply raise expectations and execution risk.

However, investors should also weigh the ongoing dilution and unprofitable track record. In light of our recent valuation report, it seems possible that Ubtech Robotics is trading beyond its estimated value.

data.title: Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de's (BMV:CHDRAUIB) Returns On Capital Are Heading Higher
data.description:
What are the early trends we should look for to identify a stock that could multiply in value over the long term? Ideally, a business will show two trends; firstly a growing return on capital employed (ROCE) and secondly, an increasing amount of capital employed. Basically this means that a company has profitable initiatives that it can continue to reinvest in, which is a trait of a compounding machine. Speaking of which, we noticed some great changes in Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de's (BMV:CHDRAUIB) returns on capital, so let's have a look.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Return On Capital Employed (ROCE): What Is It?

If you haven't worked with ROCE before, it measures the 'return' (pre-tax profit) a company generates from capital employed in its business. To calculate this metric for Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de, this is the formula:

Return on Capital Employed = Earnings Before Interest and Tax (EBIT) ÷ (Total Assets - Current Liabilities)

0.14 = Mex$15b ÷ (Mex$156b - Mex$44b) (Based on the trailing twelve months to September 2025).

Thus, Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de has an ROCE of 14%. In absolute terms, that's a pretty standard return but compared to the Consumer Retailing industry average it falls behind.

Check out our latest analysis for Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de

In the above chart we have measured Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de's prior ROCE against its prior performance, but the future is arguably more important. If you're interested, you can view the analysts predictions in our free analyst report for Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de .

The Trend Of ROCE

The trends we've noticed at Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de are quite reassuring. Over the last five years, returns on capital employed have risen substantially to 14%. Basically the business is earning more per dollar of capital invested and in addition to that, 69% more capital is being employed now too. The increasing returns on a growing amount of capital is common amongst multi-baggers and that's why we're impressed.

The Key Takeaway

A company that is growing its returns on capital and can consistently reinvest in itself is a highly sought after trait, and that's what Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de has. Since the stock has returned a staggering 345% to shareholders over the last five years, it looks like investors are recognizing these changes. In light of that, we think it's worth looking further into this stock because if Grupo Comercial Chedraui. de can keep these trends up, it could have a bright future ahead.

Before jumping to any conclusions though, we need to know what value we're getting for the current share price. That's where you can check out our FREE intrinsic value estimation for CHDRAUI B that compares the share price and estimated value.

data.title: Will Everest Group’s (EG) New Transformation-Focused CHRO Redefine Its Human Capital Narrative?
data.description:
• Everest Group, Ltd. has appointed Christopher Kujawa as Executive Vice President and Chief Human Resources Officer, effective January 20, 2026, succeeding interim CHRO Gail Van Beveren, who is staying briefly to support a smooth transition.
  • Kujawa’s track record leading large-scale HR transformation at financial and technology-enabled services firms suggests Everest is reinforcing its focus on workforce effectiveness and long-term value creation through human capital.
  • Next, we’ll explore how bringing an experienced transformation-focused CHRO onto the executive team could influence Everest Group’s investment narrative.

Advertisement

Everest Group Investment Narrative Recap

To own Everest Group, you need to be comfortable with a re/insurer that is leaning into property catastrophe risk while working to improve underwriting discipline and cost efficiency. The appointment of Christopher Kujawa as CHRO is unlikely to change near term catalysts or the biggest current risk, which still centers on catastrophe exposure and earnings volatility, but it does reinforce management’s focus on execution and workforce alignment behind its growth and risk frameworks.

This HR leadership change comes alongside a broader refresh of Everest’s executive bench, including the upcoming appointment of a new Group CFO, Elias Habayeb, in 2026. Together, these moves suggest Everest is concentrating decision making around capital allocation, risk management and people strategy at the top table, which matters for how effectively it can pursue Cat growth, scale international insurance and manage elevated expense ratios in the quarters ahead.

Yet even as Everest leans into Cat opportunities, investors should be aware of the growing exposure to more frequent and severe natural catastrophe events and...

Everest Group's narrative projects $16.8 billion revenue and $3.6 billion earnings by 2028. This implies a 1.7% yearly revenue decline but an earnings increase of about $2.8 billion from $798.0 million today.

data.title: Should Sonic’s Resilient Dividend and European Expansion Amid Softer Testing Shape ASX:SHL’s Investment Case?
data.description:
• Sonic Healthcare has recently come under renewed scrutiny as its post‑pandemic testing revenues eased and profits softened, even while it continued to invest in international pathology, imaging and general practice operations across multiple regions.
  • An important feature of this period is the company’s decades-long record of maintaining or increasing dividends and its ongoing expansion through European acquisitions, which are reshaping its earnings mix and long‑term growth profile.
  • We’ll now examine how Sonic Healthcare’s resilient dividend track record amid lower post‑COVID testing volumes affects the existing investment narrative.

We've found 12 US stocks that are forecast to pay a dividend yield of over 6% next year. See the full list for free.

Advertisement

Sonic Healthcare Investment Narrative Recap

To own Sonic Healthcare today, you need to believe in steady global demand for diagnostic services and the value of its diversified pathology and imaging footprint, despite softer post COVID testing profits. The recent share price fall and slower earnings growth have not materially altered the key near term catalyst, which remains execution on European expansion, nor the main risk, which is pressure on margins if acquisitions and higher operating costs are not offset by efficiencies.

The recent full year 2025 result, with revenue of A$9,645.45 million and net income of A$513.6 million, is central to this story because it confirms that headline growth has continued even as profit margins eased. When viewed alongside Sonic’s decision to maintain a A$0.63 dividend for the half year to June 2025, it reinforces the company’s commitment to dividends at a time when investors are questioning how resilient earnings will be as COVID related volumes normalise.

Yet investors should also be aware that heavier reliance on acquisitions is increasing operational complexity and integration risk, which...

Read the full narrative on Sonic Healthcare (it's free!)

Sonic Healthcare's narrative projects A$11.9 billion revenue and A$752.0 million earnings by 2028. This requires 7.2% yearly revenue growth and about A$238 million earnings increase from A$513.6 million today.

Uncover how Sonic Healthcare's forecasts yield a A$27.68 fair value, a 20% upside to its current price.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Seven members of the Simply Wall St Community currently value Sonic between A$24.86 and A$60.00, underlining how far opinions can spread. You should weigh these views against the risk that rising regulatory and reimbursement pressures in key markets could limit how much of Sonic’s growth potential actually reaches the bottom line, and consider several perspectives before forming your own view.

data.title: A Look At Vitalhub (TSX:VHI) Valuation As Lytham Partners Summit Appearance Draws New Attention
data.description:
Advertisement

Why Vitalhub’s Lytham Partners Summit Appearance Matters Now

Vitalhub (TSX:VHI) is set to present at the 2nd Annual Lytham Partners Healthcare Investor Summit on January 15, 2026, an event that is drawing fresh attention from investors.

This conference slot raises questions about how Vitalhub’s healthcare software portfolio and recent financial profile fit into current market expectations, especially after a year of mixed share price performance.

See our latest analysis for Vitalhub.

At a share price of CA$8.45, Vitalhub has had a softer patch recently, with a 30 day share price return of 8.55% and a 1 year total shareholder return decline of 24.22%, set against a much stronger 3 year total shareholder return of 207.27%. This suggests earlier momentum has cooled ahead of the Lytham Partners appearance.

If this healthcare summit has you thinking more broadly about the sector, it could be a good moment to scan for other ideas using healthcare stocks.

With Vitalhub’s shares softer over the past year but showing strong multi year returns, and with analyst targets and intrinsic value models sitting higher than today’s CA$8.45 price, is there a genuine opportunity here, or is the market already pricing in the company’s future growth?

Most Popular Narrative: 45.6% Undervalued

At CA$8.45 a share versus a fair value estimate of CA$15.53, the most followed narrative treats Vitalhub as materially mispriced and builds its case around healthcare digitalisation and acquisitions.

Ongoing increases in government funding for healthcare digital transformation, particularly large-scale tech budget expansions like the recent NHS investment, are providing new sales opportunities for Vitalhub's platform solutions (e.g., Intouch, Zesty, MyPathway, Synopsis). This directly supports expectations for future recurring revenue growth. The healthcare sector's accelerated move toward interoperable, cloud-based systems and digital patient management, alongside regulatory pushes for data sharing, aligns with Vitalhub's high-margin SaaS offerings and integrated platform strategy. This positioning is seen as supportive of increased net margins and ARR from SaaS subscriptions.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what kind of revenue pace, margin lift, and future earnings per share this narrative is assuming, and how that links back to today’s CA$8.45 price? The valuation depends on a specific growth runway, richer profitability, and a future earnings multiple that has to compress meaningfully from current levels. Want to see exactly how those moving parts combine into a CA$15.53 fair value and which assumptions carry the most weight?

Result: Fair Value of $15.53 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, heavy reliance on large, still-integrating acquisitions and exposure to usage-based revenues facing competition from platforms like Zoom or Teams could quickly weaken that undervalued argument.

Find out about the key risks to this Vitalhub narrative.
